Stub functions for setting a pixel and drawing a horizontal line in a graphics context.

# Purpose
This C header file declares two functions related to graphics operations. The function [`graphics_private_set_pixel`](<#graphics_private_set_pixel>) is intended to set a pixel at a specified point within a given graphics context, `GContext`. The function [`graphics_private_draw_horizontal_line_integral`](<#graphics_private_draw_horizontal_line_integral>) is designed to draw a horizontal line on a framebuffer, defined by the `GBitmap` type, between two x-coordinates at a specified y-coordinate, using a specified color, `GColor`. Both functions are declared but not implemented in this file, indicating that they are likely intended for use in other parts of the application where their implementations will be provided. The file includes a private header, `graphics_private.h`, suggesting these functions are part of a private graphics library.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/graphics/graphics_private.h`

### graphics\_private\_set\_pixel<!-- {{#callable:graphics_private_set_pixel}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_graphics_private.h#L21>)

Sets a pixel in the graphics context at a specified point.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` structure that represents the graphics context where the pixel will be set.
    - `point`: A `GPoint` structure that specifies the coordinates of the pixel to set.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - The function is currently empty and does not contain any logic to set a pixel.
- **Output**: There is no output as the function is currently empty.

### graphics\_private\_draw\_horizontal\_line\_integral<!-- {{#callable:graphics_private_draw_horizontal_line_integral}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_graphics_private.h#L23>)

Draws a horizontal line on a framebuffer at a specified y-coordinate between two x-coordinates with a given color.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` structure, which provides the drawing context.
    - `framebuffer`: A pointer to the `GBitmap` structure, which represents the framebuffer where the line will be drawn.
    - `y`: An `int16_t` value representing the y-coordinate of the horizontal line.
    - `x1`: An `int16_t` value representing the starting x-coordinate of the horizontal line.
    - `x2`: An `int16_t` value representing the ending x-coordinate of the horizontal line.
    - `color`: A `GColor` value representing the color of the line to be drawn.
- **Logic and Control Flow**: None
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
